title: Week 2 Review
html:
Congratulations on completing week two! After finishing your lessons you should now know:

  • All the hiragana modifiers
  • How to express time in the past, express directions of an action, talk about existence, how to describe something, how to express “very” and “not very” with totemo (とても) and amari (あまり) and express the number of people staying in a place
  • How to ask:
    • Someones age and replying your age

    • Ask “what kind of”, using the particle donna (どんな)

    • Ask “which one” using the expression dore deska (どれですか)

  • How to use 12 different grammar points:

    • Grammar focus on negative form of past tense Verbs = V-masen deshita (Ｖませんでした)

    • Grammar focus on verbs to go, to come, to go back (ikimas, kimas, kaerimas)

    • Grammar focus on the particle markers for action sentences (へ, で, と, も)

    • Grammar focus on the particle itsu (いつ) meaning when (when an action occurs)

    • Grammar focus on the future form

    • Grammar focus on extending an invitation V-masen ka? (Vませんか)

    • Grammar focus on adjectives in positive and negative form

    • Grammar focus on the particle soshite (そして) 

    • Grammar focus on the particle ga (が) as a marker to connect ideas that contrast

    • Grammar focus on how to conjugate an adjective with a noun

    • Grammar focus on the particle ne (ね) to express agreement/empathy

    • Grammar focus on the particles ya (や), yo (よ), ne (ね)

If you have any questions on what you have learned this week, don’t forget that you can always ask for more help in the student community!

📝
